name: Cellar
note: Cheapest food source — gives Domestic something to spend a meager turn on.
notes: Useful early-game filler; covers the slot between starting hand and Granary.

name: Wood Pile
note: Stacks of cut logs ready to burn or build with.
notes: Bare-bones production; redundant with cheap Mill but cheaper still.

name: Smokehouse
note: Cured meat keeps spirits up through winter.
notes: Combo of food + happiness at low cost makes this a strong opener.

name: Trading Post
note: Sits on the road; takes a small toll.
notes: Cheap gold trickle. Risk: dilutes Market's role.

name: Lookout Post
note: A platform with a horn and a sharp-eyed lookout.
notes: Cheap defense — gives Domestic an early way to support Foreign.

name: Tannery
note: Hides into useful goods. Smells worse than the abattoir.
notes: Generic production at low tier; OK but the deck has many of these.

name: Salt Pit
note: Salt sells anywhere there's meat to keep.
notes: Two-resource for cost 12 is generous but fits the design intent of cross-track buildings.

name: Watchtower
note: Visible safety calms the settlement.
notes: Hybrid combat/morale — niche but covers a real shape gap.

name: Hunter's Lodge
note: Game and pelts in equal measure.
notes: Mid-tier hybrid — strictly an upgrade path candidate from Cellar.

name: Stone Cutter
note: Block by block, the walls go up.
notes: Production at this cost is already crowded by the Mill family.

name: Tavern
note: Cheap drink, loud songs, fewer fights at dawn.
notes: Pure-happiness building is missing from the current set; tavern fills it perfectly.

name: Barber-Surgeon
note: The same hands that cut hair set bones.
notes: Cheap upkeep relief. Walls already does this stronger; useful as the tier-1 step.

name: Foundry
note: Heat the steel, hammer the steel, repeat.
notes: Mid-tier production — fills the slot between Mill and Workshop.

name: Apothecary
note: Tinctures the priests would frown at.
notes: Cross-track building (sci+happy) is a useful new shape.

name: Brewery
note: Beer pays in coin and good cheer.
notes: Joins Fight Circle as an alternate happiness building with a gold rider.

name: Toolworks
note: Standardized parts, faster construction.
notes: Mild Forge alternative. Risk: stacking with Forge could trivialize unit costs.

name: Town Hall
note: A roof over the council, a post for the laws.
notes: Civic flavor; mild bonuses but desirable as adjacency anchor.

name: Stables
note: Mounts ready when Foreign needs them.
notes: Generic +attack at mid cost; Barracks already does this cheaper.

name: Bakery
note: Bread, hot bread.
notes: Food-yield bump at a sensible cost; fits the upgrade tree from Cellar.

name: Smithy
note: Edges sharper, plates thicker.
notes: Symmetric combat building covers a niche Barracks/Armory doesn't.

name: Cartwright
note: Wheels that don't break.
notes: Cross-track but unflashy; another chassis for adjacency builds.

name: Hospital Annex
benefit: 1 food and decrease unit maintenance by 1
note: Convalescent rooms double as soldier billets.
notes: Mild combo; would shine if upkeep math gets harsher.

name: Customs House
note: Toll keepers with neat ledgers.
notes: Pure gold at slightly more than Bank's curve — fills the Mint/Market slot above.

name: Codex Hall
note: Shared books, shared pride.
notes: Library-adjacent at a different shape.

name: Trade Caravan
note: On the road three weeks of every four.
notes: Mid-cost dual-yield; lets Domestic feed Foreign and Chief at once.

name: Glassworks
note: Lenses and bottles, both in demand.
notes: Sci/prod hybrid is a strong new shape — encourages science investment from domestic side.

name: Granary II
note: Built atop the old one, twice as deep.
notes: Just a number bump on Granary; fine but uninspired.

name: Fortified Gate
benefit: +2 defense and decrease unit maintenance by 1
note: A wall that doubles as a barracks.
notes: Walls/Tower hybrid — earns its slot.

name: Pharmacy
note: Healing recipes, sealed in jars.
notes: Library by another name. Fine but redundant unless Library cost gets revisited.

name: Mint II
note: Stamps a heavier coin.
notes: Strict upgrade pattern; arguably better as an upgrade overlay than a new card.

name: Theater
note: Players, plays, and unstable poets.
notes: Strong dedicated happiness building; fills a gap above Fight Circle.

name: Engineering Guild
note: They calculate the load before they pour the foundation.
notes: Sci+prod at a clean cost — pivotal building for tech rushes.

name: Drill Yard
note: Daily drills with practice spears.
notes: Pairs with Barracks; reasonable mid-cost combat support.

name: Counting House
note: Ledgers thick enough to stop a knife.
notes: Gold + sci hybrid at sensible cost — fills the late-tier sci-anchor slot.

name: Mason Hall
note: Cut stone for the next twelve buildings.
notes: Workshop equivalent; fine duplicate.

name: Cathedral
note: Stained glass, taller than memory.
notes: Big-ticket happiness; an obvious late-game centerpiece.

name: Garrison
note: Beds, mess, drill — all under one roof.
notes: Sits between Walls and Tower on the upkeep curve; reasonable.

name: University Annex
note: Lecture hall and a public garden.
notes: Strong sci-leaning building; uses happiness as a counterweight to the cost.

name: Silos
note: A skyline of grain.
notes: Solid food-tier 3 building; fills slot between Mill and Hospital.

name: Refinery
note: Crude in, useful out.
notes: Strong late-mid building; production-and-gold synergy is correct.

name: Court
note: Justice as a service, taxes as a fee.
notes: Civic-tier hybrid; fills a missing slot.

name: Foundry II
note: Three crucibles burn at once.
notes: Number bump on Foundry — fine if upgrade overlay isn't enough.

name: Aqueduct
note: Clean water at every fountain.
notes: Iconic civilization building; clean cross-track yield.

name: Ironworks
note: Bigger billets, bigger blades.
notes: Late-mid Forge upgrade. Clear identity.

name: Engineer Corps
note: Sappers, surveyors, and a lot of rope.
notes: Mild defense+prod combo at the right cost.

name: Observatory
note: Stars first, scripture later.
notes: Direct upgrade slot above Library — sized correctly.

name: Warehouse
note: What can't be stored can't be sold.
notes: Generic mid-yield; fine but unflashy.

name: Temple
note: Old gods, new theories.
notes: Combines morale and sci nicely; iconic.

name: Tower II
note: Stronger walls than the first tower.
notes: Number-bump on Tower; cut if upgrades land.

name: Granaries
note: Three silos, one big roof.
notes: Strong food-tier 4.

name: Workshop II
note: Doubled benches, tripled output.
notes: Number bump only.

name: Master Smith
note: Exquisite, rare work.
notes: Endgame combat building — clean shape.

name: Drydock
note: Hulls and chassis on the same slip.
notes: Strong Foreign-supporting building; expensive but earns it.

name: Senate
note: Where the chiefs argue with words.
notes: Big civic building; supports the chief's tax flow.

name: Engineering College
note: Teach, then build it.
notes: Late-game sci/prod chassis.

name: Royal Mint
note: The coin of record.
notes: Strict upgrade-curve from Bank; clean fit.

name: Grand Bazaar
note: Every booth selling something different.
notes: Hybrid econ/morale building; great endgame chassis.

name: Citadel
benefit: +3 defense and decrease unit maintenance by 3
note: Headquarters on a hill.
notes: Late-game Foreign anchor.

name: Library II
note: Stacks higher than the average tree.
notes: Clean science upgrade slot above School.

name: Coliseum
note: Spectacle of the highest order.
notes: Pure happiness late-game; strong finisher.

name: Institute
note: Where new sciences are codified.
notes: Number bump above School/Library II — useful but redundant.

name: Palace
note: The chief's actual house.
notes: Late-game centerpiece for civic-leaning play.

name: Iron Curtain
note: A wall taller than the buildings behind it.
notes: Pure defense at endgame; clean shape.

name: Arsenal
note: Every shelf a weapon, every rack a row.
notes: Endgame Foreign chassis; well-shaped.

name: Public Works
note: Twelve crews, no idle ones.
notes: Big production tier; fine if Factory is the cap.

name: Power Plant
note: Hum and heat keep the lights on.
notes: Endgame production chassis; sci rider justifies the price.

name: Banking House
note: Where the loans live.
notes: Number-bump endgame gold building; useful.

name: Grand Hospital
note: Wings on wings on wings.
notes: Big food + morale; clean endgame fit.

name: Capitol
note: Where the laws and the loans intersect.
notes: Wonder-tier civic building.

name: Wonder of Stone
note: Everyone tells the story differently.
notes: Wonder slot — costly, identity-fix.

name: Wonder of Iron
note: A monument and a fortress in one.
notes: Wonder for combat builds.

name: Wonder of Mind
note: Carved equations on every face.
notes: Wonder for sci builds; might be too slow.

name: Wonder of Bread
note: Bread distributed daily, free.
notes: Cheap calories at endgame; less interesting than other wonders.

name: Underground Vault
note: Out of sight, mostly out of mind.
notes: Mint clone — fine duplicate.

name: Watch Mill
note: Storehouse and outpost in one.
notes: Food + defense hybrid; useful niche.

name: Beacon
note: Lit at the first sign of trouble.
notes: Cheap defense card.

name: Smelter
note: Iron and tin to ingot, fast.
notes: Forge-tier earlier; useful tempo card.

name: Hospice
note: A bed for travelers, soup for the poor.
notes: Cheap morale + food; supports civic openings.

name: Print Shop
note: Pamphlets, ledgers, broadsheets.
notes: Sci + gold hybrid; clean shape.

name: Stonemason Hall
note: Apprentices everywhere.
notes: Yet another production duplicate. Cut likely candidate.

name: Forge II
note: Specialty parts, mass production.
notes: Forge upgrade slot; well-shaped.

name: Sky Tower
note: Tall enough for the lookouts and the astronomers.
notes: Strong cross-track endgame building.

name: Granary III
note: Half the settlement's calories live here.
notes: Number bump; redundant with Hospital.

name: Civic Plaza
note: Statues, benches, occasional speeches.
notes: Mid-tier dedicated happiness; fine fill.

name: Dye Works
note: Cloth that pays double.
notes: Generic hybrid; takes a slot.

name: Soup Kitchen
note: Hot food, no questions.
notes: Cheap multi-yield — wins early-game pacing.

name: Trapper's Cabin
note: Pelts and meat in equal trade.
notes: Cheap dual-yield; reasonable.

name: Apiary
note: Bees provide more than honey.
notes: Specific, evocative; matches the tech 'bee keeping'.

name: Vineyard
note: Wine pays in cellar room and short tempers.
notes: Cross-track building; fits the 'beer brewing' tech.

name: Pottery
note: Pots for storage, jugs for sale.
notes: Generic but fits art-and-craft tier.

name: Looms
note: Cloth into clothes, looms into cloth.
notes: Maps to the 'loom' tech — useful flavor anchor.

name: Quarry Camp
note: Stone hauled out daily.
notes: Maps to 'mining' tech; flavor good, mechanic generic.

name: Engineer's House
note: She lives where the project lives.
notes: Mid-tier sci/prod chassis.

name: Public Bath
note: Steam, gossip, clean people.
notes: Tier 1 dedicated morale.

name: Generator Shed
note: First electric lights in the settlement.
notes: Maps to 'Generators' tech; clean shape and fits the post-apoc theme.

name: Solar Array
note: Panels squint at the dust.
notes: Endgame upgrade above Generator Shed; great.

name: Recycling Yard
note: One person's scrap is another's chassis.
notes: Maps to 'Recycling' tech; mechanically appropriate.

name: Grand Theater
note: Sold out, every season.
notes: Endgame happiness chassis; clean curve.

name: Fortress
benefit: +5 defense and decrease unit maintenance by 3
note: A keep thick enough to last a siege.
notes: Endgame Foreign chassis.

name: Council Chamber
note: Argument is, after all, a kind of research.
notes: Mid-tier civic-academic hybrid.
